facade_description: 32B Oxford St presents a house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows. HCD note: The property has value as a late-nineteenth- to early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian and early-twentieth-century architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s earl
